I was very unsure what was intended here—a video would be very helpful. The scenario that seems most likely is that the child is complaining about not having cream (nonpossession)—this is supported by the father saying that "that's Bwww's cream." Another option is that the child is prohibiting the parent from giving them (what they think is) cream or rejecting eating cream. The prohibition possibility is made less likely by the mother initially not understanding what the child meant to say (suggesting that cream/milk wasn't being served at the time). Nonexistence is another possibility (if the child initially thought that nobody had cream).
